summary: Volume measures 3D space (length x width x height style formulas); surface area adds up the area of every face.
explanation: A rectangular solid's volume is length times width times height; its surface area is the sum of the areas of all 6 faces, which come in 3 pairs of equal rectangles. A cube is just a rectangular solid where all edges are equal. A cylinder's volume is the area of its circular base times its height (\(\pi r^2 h\)); its surface area is the two circular bases plus the rectangular "label" that wraps around the side (\(2\pi rh\)). The main diagonal of a rectangular solid (corner to opposite corner) can be found with a 3D extension of the Pythagorean theorem: \(d=\sqrt{l^2+w^2+h^2}\). Always double-check units and make sure you're answering what's asked — volume and surface area questions are easy to mix up under time pressure.
